description: A Future is constructed eagerly so its side effect fires immediately, before the intended composition or sequencing.
body: Failure mode: code writes val f = Future { sideEffect() } expecting it to be a recipe, but Future runs on creation, so the effect fires at construction and any later sequencing or retry logic operates on an already-executed, memoized result. Detection signals: side effects happen earlier than the for-comprehension that 'sequences' them; retries re-use a cached result instead of re-running; multiple Futures built before a for run in parallel unintentionally. Do not treat a Future as lazy. Safer fallback: defer construction (a def, or wrap in IO/Task which is lazy), build the program first and run it once at the edge, and test that no effect fires until execution.
